solute.description: This molecule, 4-nitrophenylacetic acid, consists of a central benzene ring with substituents at the para (1,4) positions. It features a carboxylic acid group attached to the ring via a single methylene bridge and a nitro group bonded directly to the opposite side of the aromatic core. The structure is highly polar and possesses significant hydrogen bonding capacity, acting as both a hydrogen bond donor and acceptor through the carboxylic acid and nitro moieties. This small organic molecule is characterized by the electronic contrast between the strongly electron-withdrawing nitro group and the acidic carboxyl group separated by a rigid aromatic spacer.

solvent.description: This molecule, $N,N$-dimethylacetamide, is a small acyclic tertiary amide consisting of an acetyl group bonded to a dimethylamine moiety. The structure features a central carbonyl carbon double-bonded to oxygen and single-bonded to a methyl group and a nitrogen atom, which is further substituted with two methyl groups. It is a highly polar molecule characterized by a significant dipole moment originating from the amide resonance, yet it lacks hydrogen bond donors due to the absence of N-H bonds. Consequently, it functions exclusively as a hydrogen bond acceptor through its carbonyl oxygen within a compact, four-carbon framework.
